skill.name: clinical-reports
skill.description: Write comprehensive clinical reports (case reports, diagnostic reports, clinical trial reports, and patient documentation) when accuracy, regulatory compliance (HIPAA/FDA/ICH-GCP), and template-driven validation are required.
skill.summary: Template-driven clinical report authoring with built-in regulatory compliance checking and HIPAA de-identification validation.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 563 potential issues across 31 files (10,980 lines). All HIGH-severity 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are false positives — the flagged patterns are regex-based text validators for HIPAA identifier detection and medical terminology checking, not cryptographic operations. All 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are false positives from shell commands shown in markdown documentation as usage examples. The Python scripts perform legitimate file I/O for report validation, data extraction, and template generation. One minor bug found: json.dumps() used instead of json.dump() in validate_case_report.py line 319, causing JSON output to not write correctly when --output flag is used. No credential access, malicious network calls, or command injection vectors present.

medium_findings.title: Potential JSON serialization bug in validate_case_report.py
medium_findings.description: json.dumps() used instead of json.dump() at line 319, which serializes to string but does not write to the file handle. The JSON output is silently discarded when the --output flag is used.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct code inspection confirms json.dumps(report, f, indent=2) should be json.dump(report, f, indent=2) - this is a definite bug that makes --output flag non-functional.

low_findings.title: Misclassified regex patterns as cryptographic algorithms
low_findings.description: Over 200 static analysis findings labeled 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' are actually regular expression patterns used for HIPAA identifier detection, medical terminology validation, and text parsing. No actual cryptographic operations are performed. All findings are false positives.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: 100% confident these are false positives - direct code review confirms all flagged patterns are regex validators, not cryptographic functions.

low_findings.title: Misclassified shell commands as backtick execution
low_findings.description: Over 200 static analysis findings labeled 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' are actually shell commands shown in markdown code blocks as documentation examples. These are instructions for users to run Python scripts, not executable backtick-injected commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: 100% confident these are false positives - all are shell commands within markdown fenced code blocks serving as documentation, not runtime code execution.

low_findings.title: Misclassified 'System/Network reconnaissance' pattern matches
low_findings.description: Findings labeled 'System reconnaissance' and 'Network reconnaissance' are triggered by medical terminology and anatomical references in template files, plus citation URLs in reference documents. No actual reconnaissance behavior exists.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: 100% confident these are false positives - content is medical text and citation URLs in documentation, not system reconnaissance commands.

low_findings.title: Misclassified Python file write operations
low_findings.description: Five static findings labeled 'Python file write/append' or 'Python shutil operations' are legitimate file I/O for CLI tools that save validation reports and copy templates. No unauthorized file access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All file operations are parameterized via CLI arguments (--output flag) and only write to user-specified paths. Standard CLI tool behavior.

low_findings.title: Misclassified certificate/key file references
low_findings.description: Static analysis findings labeled 'Certificate/key files' are from Python scripts that reference certificate files in medical regulatory compliance contexts, not actual credential handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No actual credential or certificate file access. References are related to regulatory documentation compliance checks.

low_findings.title: Heuristic 'dangerous combination' finding is false positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer heuristic flagged a 'Dangerous combination: Code execution + Network + Credential access' pattern, but this is an aggregation artifact from individual false positive patterns. No actual code execution, malicious network calls, or credential access exists in the codebase.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Thorough code review of all 8 Python scripts confirms no credential handling, no network requests at runtime, and no dynamic code execution. The heuristic triggered on aggregated false positive patterns.

content.user_title: Generate clinical reports with regulatory compliance checks
content.value_statement: Medical professionals spend hours formatting clinical reports and checking regulatory compliance. This skill provides ready-to-use templates for case reports, diagnostic reports, and trial documentation with automated HIPAA de-identification and CARE guideline validation.
actual_capabilities: Generate CARE-aligned clinical case reports with proper section structure and consent documentation
Produce structured diagnostic reports for radiology, pathology, and laboratory results
Validate clinical reports against HIPAA Safe Harbor de-identification requirements
Check regulatory compliance with FDA, HIPAA, and ICH-GCP documentation standards
Format adverse event data into clinical trial summary tables
Validate ICH E3 compliance for Clinical Study Reports
limitations: Does not replace medical professional review or institutional approval processes
HIPAA identifier detection uses pattern matching and may miss context-specific identifiers
Scripts require Python 3.10+ and manual execution via command line
Does not connect to EHR systems or automate data extraction from medical databases

use_cases.title: Clinical researcher preparing case reports
use_cases.description: A clinical researcher drafts a journal-ready case report following CARE guidelines. They use the template and validation script to ensure all required sections are present and HIPAA identifiers are flagged before submission.

use_cases.title: Regulatory affairs specialist compiling trial documentation
use_cases.description: A regulatory specialist prepares a Clinical Study Report (CSR) aligned with ICH E3 standards. They use the CSR template, format adverse event data, and run compliance checks before regulatory submission.

use_cases.title: Clinician documenting patient encounters
use_cases.description: A physician creates SOAP notes, history and physical reports, and discharge summaries using structured templates. The built-in quality checklists ensure completeness and regulatory readiness.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a clinical case report
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to write a clinical case report following CARE guidelines. Use the case_report_template.md from the assets folder as a starting point. The patient is a [age]-year-old [sex] with [condition]. Include sections for introduction, patient information, clinical findings, timeline, diagnostic assessment, therapeutic interventions, follow-up and outcomes, discussion, and informed consent. After drafting, validate it using the validate_case_report.py script.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a submission-ready case report for a medical journal

prompt_templates.title: Run HIPAA de-identification check
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the check_deidentification.py script on my clinical report file at [path/to/report.md]. Check for all 18 HIPAA Safe Harbor identifiers including names, dates, geographic details, contact information, and medical record numbers. Show me a summary of any violations found and recommendations for remediation.
prompt_templates.scenario: Ensuring patient privacy compliance before publication

prompt_templates.title: Format adverse event data for clinical trial report
prompt_templates.prompt: I have adverse event data in a CSV file at [path/to/ae_data.csv]. Use the format_adverse_events.py script to generate a summary table with columns for treatment arm, total patients, any AE, related AE, serious AE, deaths, and discontinuations. Include percentage calculations for each category.
prompt_templates.scenario: Preparing safety data tables for a Clinical Study Report

prompt_templates.title: Validate ICH E3 compliance for a CSR
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the validate_trial_report.py script on my Clinical Study Report at [path/to/csr.md]. Check that all ICH E3 required sections are present: title page, synopsis, table of contents, ethics section, study objectives, patient information, efficacy and safety evaluations, discussion, references, and appendices. Report the compliance rate and any missing sections.
prompt_templates.scenario: Ensuring regulatory submission readiness for a clinical trial report

output_examples.input: Run HIPAA de-identification check on a case report with patient data including names, dates, and addresses.
output: Recommendation: Remove or redact 12 identified HIPAA identifiers.

output_examples.input: Validate a clinical case report against CARE guidelines.
output: Recommendation: Add timeline section with key clinical events and dates.

output_examples.input: Generate an adverse events summary table from CSV data with two treatment arms.

best_practices: Run HIPAA de-identification checks before sharing any report outside your institution to avoid privacy violations
Use the CARE validation script early in the writing process to catch missing sections before final review
Store all clinical reports under version control to maintain audit trails and track changes over time
anti_patterns: Skipping the de-identification step before publication. Always run check_deidentification.py even on de-identified data to catch missed identifiers
Editing templates directly in the assets folder. Copy templates to a separate working directory before making report-specific changes
Running validation scripts only at the end of report writing. Validate early and often to reduce rework on missing sections

faq.question: What types of clinical reports can this skill generate?
faq.answer: This skill supports case reports (CARE guidelines), radiology reports, pathology reports, laboratory reports, SOAP notes, history and physical exams, discharge summaries, consultation notes, SAE reports, and Clinical Study Reports (ICH E3).

faq.question: Does this skill connect to my hospital's EHR system?
faq.answer: No. This skill works with markdown files and Python scripts that you run locally. It does not connect to any external system or database.

faq.question: How does HIPAA de-identification checking work?
faq.answer: The check_deidentification.py script scans report text for all 18 HIPAA Safe Harbor identifiers using regex pattern matching. It flags names, dates, geographic details, contact information, and other identifying data.

faq.question: What Python version do I need to run the scripts?
faq.answer: The scripts require Python 3.10 or higher. All dependencies are from the Python standard library (argparse, json, re, pathlib, csv), so no additional package installation is needed.

faq.question: Can I use these templates for regulatory submissions?
faq.answer: The templates provide structure aligned with CARE guidelines and ICH E3 standards, but they are starting points. You must review and customize content for your specific regulatory requirements and institutional policies.

faq.question: What is the difference between assets and references folders?
faq.answer: The assets folder contains editable report templates that you copy and fill in. The references folder contains guideline documents that provide background standards and best practices for writing clinical reports.
